title: carmichaelia muritai
intro: Carmichaelia muritai, also known as the Mount Pirongia spine flower, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in Maori herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or infused in hot water to make a tea, and it is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
